The article features a range of Instagram accounts covering different workout styles suited to different types of exercisers."

    answer: "Yes - the article specifically curated Instagram workouts designed to be done in the comfort of your own home. The picks are organised by exercise preference, so whether you prefer yoga, strength training or something else, there is an option suited to your style."
---

## If You’re A Yoga Lover

Founder of wellness protocol Body By Finch (as well as supplements range, Kissed Earth, [which we interviewed her about here](https://www.instagram.com/tv/B9m5BrZlpkK/?hl=en)), [Rachael Finch](https://www.beauticate.com/who/actors-presenters/rachel-finch-model-and-tv-presenter/) knows her stuff when it comes to health. Rachael shared her 15 minute yoga flow (designed to be repeated to meet your daily 30 minute exercise target) to her legions of followers and it’s a beauty – it’ll get your heart pumping, while still being gentle and soothing – exactly what’s needed in these crazy times.

## If You Like Your Workouts Dance-Inspired

Dancing has the happy advantage of being both fun and technically counting as a workout (win, win, win), so if you like to exercise while pretending you’re not actually exercising, this dance-inspired cardio session from American trainer Aubre Winters would be perfect for you. It’s super fun – and effective.

## If You’re A HIIT Addict

Can’t quit the HIIT? Psycle, the cult fitness establishment that counts Victoria Beckham and Millie Mackintosh amongst its fans, have been sharing a steady stream of routines designed for exercising at home. This Fit In 45 routine combines elements of HIIT and strength – and means you can get your sweat sesh over and done with in half an hour.

## If You’re Looking For A Supermodel-Approved Session

Kiwi supermodel and Victoria Secret alumnae Georgia Fowler ([whose gorgeous sister Kate we recently interviewed](https://www.beauticate.com/who/models/why-this-model-mum-of-two-doesnt-own-a-hair-dryer/)) shared the actual lower-body workout that she swears by (!!!) to look as good as she does. Her 12 minute video is designed to be repeated three times, and while it’s certainly not for the faint of heart, it’ll help you look and feel runway ready. Watch and repeat.

## If You’re Pregnant

As their Instagram handle suggests, Knocked Up Fitness is dedicated to providing fitness advice to those both pre-and post-partum. This workout is perfect if you’re looking to get your total body fitness fix while pregnant.

## If You’re Looking For A Good Stretch

A good stretching session is perfect for supplementing your existing workout roster, or to ease you back into exercising if you’re a little rusty. UK trainer Zanna van Dijk shares the stretching routine she swears by to start the day feeling loosened up and relaxed (it’s also a fab little routine to tack onto the back of another workout to help cool down).

## If You Want Fab Glutes

Looking to strengthen your lower body in particular? This video from Barre Body's [Emma Seibold](https://www.beauticate.com/who/entrepreneurs/emma-seibold-fitness-entrepreneur/) delivers a quick and effective routine you can do anywhere – with seriously good results. Repeat for maximum effect as many times as you can manage, or pair with another [Barre Body workout](https://barrebody.com.au/).

## If Your Pets Want To Get Involved Too

If you’ve ever tried exercising at home with a pet, you’re probably had a few incidents of being pawed for attention by your pup while perfecting your Downward Facing Dog, or having your cat jeopardise your pushups by lying down in the middle of your yoga mat. Well, PT Sohee Lee has devised the perfect pet-friendly workout routine: should appropriately-sized pugs or chihuahuas not be available, may I suggest you swap in kettlebells or weights as needed.*Story by Tess de Vivie de Régie. Holding shot image from @sportyandrich*
